untranslatable_reason: Bump-and-Run Reversal is a visual chart pattern requiring peak/trough detection, shape classification (rounded vs pointed), and multi-bar structure analysis. This cannot be expressed using only the allowed point-in-time indicators (price, moving averages, RSI, ATR, etc.). The pattern identification requires pattern recognition logic not available in this rule engine. Attempted approximation using price breakdowns and moving average crosses, but this does not capture the true bump structure validation.
translation_notes: Signal describes a complex reversal pattern that requires identifying: (1) prior uptrend establishment, (2) bump formation with specific shape characteristics, (3) breakout direction. Entry conditions approximate a breakout below recent structure, but cannot validate the preceding bump geometry. The 76% success rate in bear markets suggests the pattern is statistically valid, but mechanically translating it requires pattern detection algorithms beyond this rule schema.
